query:
so.. we'll save that for another day.  back to the crt-034 completion: issue 459: here's the scope and questions.. thoughts? 1. Modify `run_co_access_promotion_tick` to write both `(entry_id_a, entry_id_b)` and
   `(entry_id_b, entry_id_a)` as distinct `CoAccess`-typed edges in `GRAPH_EDGES` for each
   qualifying pair going forward.
2. Add a one-time schema migration (v18→v19) that back-fills the missing reverse edge for
   every existing forward-only CoAccess edge whose `source = 'co_access'` in `GRAPH_EDGES`
   (covers both bootstrap-era and crt-034 tick-era edges).
3. Confirm via a Unimatrix knowledge update to entry #3830 that ADR-006's follow-up contract
   is fulfilled: forward-edge layout was intentional, back-fill scope is bounded by
   `source = 'co_access'` on `GRAPH_EDGES`, and cycle detection is unaffected. Here's the SCOPE.md for your review. Three open
  questions need your input before I proceed to Phase
  1b:

  Q2 — back-fill created_by field: copy from forward
  edge ('bootstrap'/'tick') or use a new 'back-fill'
  sentinel?

  Q3 — tick log semantics: report as individual edge
  writes (up to 2× pairs) or pair promotions?

  Q3 (OQ4) — AC-12 PPR test: extend existing
  TypedGraphState tests or write new graph traversal
  tests? (Researcher flagged this as needing
  verification.)

  The scope itself is clean — tight boundaries, clear
  AC-01 through AC-14, no surprises beyond the gotcha
  that back-fill must filter by source = 'co_access'
  alone (not created_by = 'bootstrap'), because tick-era
   forward-only edges also need the reverse written. this is crt-035

entries.title: ADR-006 crt-034/crt-035: Edge Directionality — v1 One Direction, crt-035 Fulfills Back-fill Contract
Back-filling reverse GRAPH_EDGES for symmetric relations: filter by source, not created_by
co_access promotion SELECT missing quarantine-status filter causes compaction/promotion oscillation
ADR-002 crt-034: Constants Location — unimatrix-store/src/read.rs alongside EDGE_SOURCE_NLI
